ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific entry for 2-in-1 less-sugar premix variant. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers coffee and coffee substitutes broadly; no specific 2-in-1 premix less-sugar subcode exists. Closest defensible parent code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34658001 refers to coffee as a substance; no specific instant premix 2-in-1 less-sugar concept exists. Closest defensible match used.

serving.description: 1 sachet prepared as directed (approximately 200 ml beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one sachet (approximately 12–14 g powder) dissolved in 200 ml of hot water, yielding a ready-to-drink cup of about 200 g.

nutrient_source.title: 2 in 1 Black Coffee (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '2 in 1 black coffee (less sugar)', record ID D09010003. All per-100g values are as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 2-in-1 less-sugar instant black coffee. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the analogous food 'instant coffee with sugar' from the University of Sydney GI Database. The carbohydrate content is predominantly sucrose (GI ~65) diluted by the low overall carbohydrate density of the beverage as prepared. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 10.76) / 100 ≈ 5.9, using 200 g serving size with 5.38 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (6 mg/100 g) and low sugar (3.36 g/100 g); caffeine content warrants moderation
hypertension.guidance: Sodium is negligible at 6 mg/100 g and poses no hypertension risk; however, caffeine intake should be limited to no more than 1–2 servings per day as excess caffeine (>300 mg/day) can acutely elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Contains added sugar (3.36 g/100 g); classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage premix regardless of reduced-sugar formulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although sugar content (3.36 g/100 g) is below the 5 g/100 g beverage threshold and the glycaemic load per serving is moderate (~5.9), this product is a sweetened instant drink mix and individuals with type 2 diabetes should monitor cumulative daily sugar intake from all beverages and prefer unsweetened black coffee where possible.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat (0.04 g/100 g), no saturated fat, very low sodium; chlorogenic acids in coffee may confer modest cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The near-zero fat and very low sodium profile present no cardiovascular risk from this beverage; moderate coffee consumption (1–2 cups/day) is associated with neutral to modestly beneficial cardiovascular outcomes, but added sugar should be accounted for in total daily intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chlorogenic acids and polyphenols in instant coffee have evidence-based anti-inflammatory properties; low sugar and negligible fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Instant coffee polyphenols may modestly support anti-inflammatory pathways; limit to 1–2 cups daily to avoid excess caffeine which can disrupt sleep and worsen RA symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 2 in 1 Black Coffee (Less Sugar) is a Singaporean instant beverage product consisting of spray-dried or freeze-dried instant coffee granules pre-blended with a reduced quantity of sugar, packaged in individual sachets. It is prepared by dissolving the sachet contents in approximately 200 ml of hot water, yielding a ready-to-drink black coffee with a mild sweetness. Unlike the standard 3-in-1 coffee mixes that include non-dairy creamer, this 2-in-1 formulation contains only coffee and sugar, making it lower in fat and calories. Per 100 g of prepared beverage, it provides 22 kcal, 0.19 g protein, 0.04 g fat, 5.38 g carbohydrate (of which 3.36 g is sugar), 0.14 g dietary fibre, and 6 mg sodium. The beverage is ultra-processed but low in calories and virtually fat-free. It is widely consumed in Singapore as a convenient morning or afternoon coffee option. Clinically, the low sodium and low fat profile make it suitable for most adults; however, the added sugar classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage premix, and individuals with type 2 diabetes or those monitoring glycaemic intake should account for cumulative sugar from all beverages. Caffeine content warrants moderation for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular conditions. The chlorogenic acids and polyphenols naturally present in coffee may confer modest anti-inflammatory and insulin-sensitising benefits.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record D09010003), which is the primary approved regional source for Singapore food records. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and consistent with the food type. Confidence is high as an exact match was found in the primary approved source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this instant premix beverage are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D09010003 as of 2026. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 in the HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo D09010003). saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per HPB source given total fat of only 0.04 g/100 g. GI is an analogous estimate based on instant coffee with sugar from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific less-sugar 2-in-1 formulation was found. This product is classified as ultra-processed (sweetened instant drink mix) and as a sugar-sweetened beverage premix for clinical classification purposes despite the reduced-sugar formulation.
